supplies.title: Household Items - Art: Fast Track
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
- Cloth (T1/2/3)
- Scrap Cardboard for base of clay (T1)
- Paper towels (T1)
- 12" x 18" white paper (can be drawing, watercolor, or pastel paper) (T1)
- Scrap paper (T1/2)
- Houseplant. Grocery stores often have potted herbs in the produce section. Or pull up a weed and plant it in a cup with dirt from your yard. (T2)
- Manipulatives/found objects (can include pebbles, leaves, Tangram pieces, beans, math counters, Legos, anything that you have enough of to repeat a pattern) (T2)
- Markers or crayons (T2)
- Tea cup and saucer (can use mug and small plate) used for model (T2)
- Basket with handle used for model (T2)
- Lantern used for model. It's on pg.14 of Drawing, Design, and Craftwork. You can use a can with a funnel on top to recreate it. Or make it out of paper. (T2)
- Cylindrical objects of 3 different sizes (cans, candles, vases) (T2)
- Conical objects like flower pot, umbrella, tea pitcher, funnel, etc. Preferably at least 3. (T2)
- 6x6 square piece of paper in any color (T2)
- An item that is an open circle (mason jar lid ring, embroidery hoop, hula hoop) (T2)
- Circle template. Trace around any object at least 4 inches in diameter: bowl, plate, lid, etc., on a sturdy piece of cardboard or cardstock. Cut it out. (T2)
- Canned good (T2)
- Towel (T1/2/3)

books.title: Household Items - Art: Fast Track
books.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
- Cloth (T1/2/3)
- Scrap Cardboard for base of clay (T1)
- Paper towels (T1)
- 12" x 18" white paper (can be drawing, watercolor, or pastel paper) (T1)
- Scrap paper (T1/2)
- Houseplant. Grocery stores often have potted herbs in the produce section. Or pull up a weed and plant it in a cup with dirt from your yard. (T2)
- Manipulatives/found objects (can include pebbles, leaves, Tangram pieces, beans, math counters, Legos, anything that you have enough of to repeat a pattern) (T2)
- Markers or crayons (T2)
- Tea cup and saucer (can use mug and small plate) used for model (T2)
- Basket with handle used for model (T2)
- Lantern used for model. It's on pg.14 of Drawing, Design, and Craftwork. You can use a can with a funnel on top to recreate it. Or make it out of paper. (T2)
- Cylindrical objects of 3 different sizes (cans, candles, vases) (T2)
- Conical objects like flower pot, umbrella, tea pitcher, funnel, etc. Preferably at least 3. (T2)
- 6x6 square piece of paper in any color (T2)
- An item that is an open circle (mason jar lid ring, embroidery hoop, hula hoop) (T2)
- Circle template. Trace around any object at least 4 inches in diameter: bowl, plate, lid, etc., on a sturdy piece of cardboard or cardstock. Cut it out. (T2)
- Canned good (T2)
- Towel (T1/2/3)

supplies.title: Household Items - General Science: Grade 5
supplies.note:
Typically easy for students to scavenge or teachers to obtain locally:
• honey
• food items suggested
• spoons
• small saucepan
• plant fertilizer of your choice (only a small amount)
• water
• flowers (Term 1)
• a strainer or sieve
• 1 c dry soil
• kitchen scale
• kitchen thermometer, any stick-type tool that can be placed into hot water and soil
• oven
• oven mitt
• clock
• chair or stepladder
• stopwatch, such as found on most electronic devices
• freezer
• stove
• 1-2 c white vinegar
• 1/2-1 c baking soda
• flat surface outside to launch rocket
• 10-20' height reference, such as the side of a building, a long piece or wood, or a flagpole
• various supplies by student design
• printables
• optional: barometer
• optional: computer

supplies.note: Safety goggles are included in the HST Alveary kits for grades 5 and high school Chemistry.

supplies.qty: 1 per student (If students are not doing lab at the same time, they could share.)

books.title: Scale/Balance
books.rationale: Students may use most any common kitchen scale/balance for science during the younger years, but at some point before high school a more accurate, precise, and sensitive scale should be obtained.
books.maySub: any reliable scale that goes down to 0.01g.

books.title: Small Collection Containers
books.rationale: Take 1-2 outside on nature walks to collect specimens.  Sometimes used in science beginning in grade 3; students need 8 for lab in grade 8.
books.maySub: any recycled containers to collect items, such as jam, pesto, or sun-dried tomato jars; pick a regularly used pantry item and collect over time.

books.title: Student Microscope
books.rationale: To observe very small objects beginning in Grade 4. Especially needed in high school Biology, Botany, and Microbiology.
books.note: We recommend that the microscope be accessible for Grades 4+, as the tool helps cultivate scientific curiosity.
books.maySub: any microscope with similar specifications.
